title: Stock Market Today, June 17: Nu Holdings Rises as AI Credit Models Lift Lending Outlook
description: Nu Holdings’ AI models are moving deeper into credit decisions, raising the stakes for whether lending growth can stay profitable.

title: It’s ‘unavoidable’: Apple says it will be forced to raise prices due to the AI boom
description: Tech giants are gobbling up memory chips for AI servers, leaving Apple with soaring component costs that Tim Cook says will inevitably be passed on to consumers.

title: Protestors say no to AI data centers in Jefferson City
description: The protest was organized by the Missouri Rural Crisis Center.

title: Vietnam workers keenest on AI in South-east Asia; Singapore employees among most sceptical: survey
description: The Republic’s labour force doubts AI’s productive capacity, and is wary about its impact on job security

title: Commentary: Anthropic’s retreat is a win for Chinese open-source AI
description: Cheaper AI was already a major selling point for Chinese labs, and now they can add that they’re more reliable, says Catherine Thorbecke for Bloomberg Opinion.
